office_does: The county council adopts budgets and policy and oversees county services, infrastructure, and land-use decisions.
race_blurb: Three candidates bring school, ferry, nonprofit, health-care, emergency-service, and environmental backgrounds.

candidates.summary: Swanson is a former school-board member and superintendent and current ferry-advisory chair emphasizing reserves, housing, ferries, and stewardship.
highlights: Emphasizes rebuilding reserves, housing options, ferry reliability, and stewardship [S1].

spending.basis: Rebuilding financial reserves supplies a modest restraint and accountability direction.

experience.basis: Prior elected school-board service and current ferry leadership establish a public record.

candidates.summary: Ingman is a nonprofit manager with housing, food-access, health-care, ferry, and youth experience emphasizing housing, business, ferries, environment, transparency, and balanced budgets.
highlights: Emphasizes housing, local business, ferry reliability, environmental protection, and balanced budgets [S1].

spending.basis: Her platform genuinely mixes balanced budgets with public-service and housing priorities.

experience.basis: She has broad nonprofit and service experience but no documented elected record.

candidates.summary: Koplan is a health-care and school professional with climate, emergency-service, hospice, and wildlife service emphasizing collaboration and environmental protection.
highlights: Has climate, fire and EMS, oil-spill, hospice, and wildlife service [S1].

climate.basis: Her climate and environmental service suggests an action orientation, though no energy-policy mechanism is stated.

experience.basis: She has broad public-service experience but no documented elected record.
